Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Source Material A detailed description of a complex multinational supply chain for smartphone manufacturing RD to Sourcing to Assembly to Logistics to Distribution to Sales Support Goal Generate 20 thought provoking open ended questions Focus Areas Insights potential issues strategies for improvement implications of decisions Constraint No SQL queries Just the list of questions

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Source Text Key Themes and Components Phase 1 RD and Sourcing Design finalization multi country sourcing Taiwan Japan S Korea Germany US lead times quality metrics contractual terms Phase 2 Manufacturing Malaysia assembly plant 24 7 shifts automation human labor QA testing packing Phase 3 Logistics and Distribution Air vs Sea freight global partners customs regulatory compliance JIT vs Aggregated shipping high demand vs slow moving markets regional DCs Europe NA Asia LatAm Phase 4 Last Mile and Retail Warehouses retailers e commerce D2C VIP Pre order Phase 5 Planning and Tech Inventory tracking sales forecasting order adjustment digital systems POs invoices scheduling data analytics ML Phase 6 Broader Context Currency time zones regulations environmental labor compliance warranty recalls continuous improvement cost time carbon

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Brainstorming Questions by Category Category A Resilience and Risk Management The What if scenarios What if a key supplier eg Taiwan fab goes down Geopolitical risks Single point of failure analysis Impact of currency fluctuation on margin

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Category B Operational Efficiency and Optimization Balancing Air fast expensive vs Sea slow cheap When is the tipping point JIT in high demand markets vs safety stock risks Automation vs Labor in Malaysia cost flexibility trade off

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Category C Data and Technology Digital Twin AI Accuracy of ML forecasting vs black swan events Integration of data between disparate logistics partners Real time visibility gaps

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Category D Sustainability and Ethics ESG Carbon footprint of air freight vs customer expectation of speed Labor compliance across different jurisdictions Circular economy recalls warranty end of life

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Category E Strategic Alignment and Market Dynamics How does the VIP D2C model conflict with traditional retailer relationships Adapting to regulatory changes eg new tariffs

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Drafting and Refining the 20 Questions Iterative Process Draft 1 Sourcing How do we manage risk with suppliers in Taiwan Refinement Given the heavy reliance on specific geographic regions like Taiwan for microprocessors for critical components what specific China plus one or nearshoring strategies could mitigate geopolitical or natural disaster risks without significantly increasing costs

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft 2 Logistics When should we use planes vs ships Refinement Considering the trade off between air freight speed cost and cargo ships cost carbon footprint how can dynamic routing algorithms be better utilized to decide the optimal mode of transport based on real time demand volatility rather than static rules

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft 3 Manufacturing Is 24 7 work sustainable Refinement With the assembly plant operating 24 7 what are the diminishing returns regarding worker fatigue and equipment maintenance downtime and how does this impact the overall defect rate during quality checks

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft 4 Data Is the AI good enough Refinement While machine learning models are used for demand planning how does the system account for bullwhip effects caused by panic buying or viral social media trends that historical data might not accurately predict

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft 5 Compliance How do we handle laws everywhere Refinement As environmental and labor regulations diverge between the EU North America and Southeast Asia how can the company standardize its compliance reporting to avoid reputational damage while maintaining operational agility

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction during drafting Make sure they are open ended How To what extent What are the implications rather than Yes No

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Continuing to generate questions covering all sections

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Focus on Inventory The prompt mentions JIT for high demand and aggregated for low demand Question Does this split create inventory imbalance if a slow market suddenly becomes hot

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Focus on Quality Components come from everywhere Who is liable if a German sensor fails in a phone sold in Brazil Traceability issues

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Focus on Cost Currency Multi currency hedging strategies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Constraints 20 questions Yes Thought provoking Open ended Yes Based on text Yes No SQL Yes

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Polish Formatting Present as a clean list Proceeding to generate output
